title: Cupar Banking Hub Petition | Wendy Chamberlain MP for North East Fife
text_excerpt: --> Cupar Banking Hub Petition | Wendy Chamberlain MP for North East Fife top of page About News Constituency Local Funding Options Cupar Banking Hub Petition Westminster Visiting Westminster Getting Help Contact Me - Constituents Contact me - Westminster Roles Useful Contacts FAQs More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Cupar Banking Hub Petition Petition for a banking hub in Cupar Everyone should be able to access cash and banking advice when they need it. This should not be restricted by poor mobile or broadband connectivity; not having easy access to a phone; or preferring to do transactions face to face. Almost 14,000 people live in the area surrounding Cupar, although the town serves many more in the surrounding villages. Nonetheless Cupar is served by just one building society following the closure of multiple local bank branches. It is not feasible for many people to travel outwith Cupar to neighbouring towns such as St Andrews in light of cuts to bus routes. We believe that all the residents of Cupar and the surrounding area would benefit from a cash or banking hub. A cash hub would allow consumers and businesses to access and bank cash safely. A banking hub would provide vital face to face services and advice for our community. Please sign the petition if you agree: First name * Last name * Email * Address * Sign Petition Unit G1, Granary Business Centre Coal Road Cupar KY15 5YQ Constituency: 01334 656361 Parliament: 0207 219 4409 wendy.chamberlain.mp@parliament.uk Privacy Notice: Details about our data protection and privacy policies are in Wendy Chamberlain’s Privacy Policy. bottom of page
